title: David's Place Was Empty
summary: “And the king sat upon his seat, as at other times, even upon a seat by the wall: and Jonathan arose, and Abner sat by Saul's side, and David's place was empty. And it came to pass on the morrow, which was the second day of the month, that David's place was empty: and Saul said unto Jonathan his son, Wherefore cometh not the son of Jesse to meat, neither yesterday, nor to day?” 1Sam 20:25, 27 “Noone loved the house of Godand the fellowship of the people of God more than David. “[[A Song of degrees of David.]] I was glad when they said unto me, Let us go into the house of the LORD”(Psalm 122:1). In the scripture passage above, the occasion was a festival of the new moon. This was a holy day, quite similar to the Sabbath, when all were expected to participate, be ceremoniallyclean, and offer up sacrifices.“And in the beginnings of your months ye shall offer a burnt offering unto the LORD; two young bullocks, and one ram, seven lambs of the first year without spot”(Num 28:11). For all these reasons, no one expected the seat of David to be empty. Anempty seat conveys a powerful message, especially when your name is David. Even the King, Saul, paid attention and was disturbed.May we serve the Lord Jesus so effectively that Kings and courtiers will take notice when our seats are empty. The tomb of Jesus was and is empty, and the entire worldpaid attention. It was the empty tomb that raised the question: who is Jesus and why did he die? The empty seat of David also raised many questions. Saints of God, my main aim in this message today is to ask you to humbly consider the meaning of your seat in the Kingdom of the Lord Jesus Christ.
